answer:
Sure, let me think back to a time when I had to give a presentation in class. Actually, last semester in my history class, I had to do a presentation about the Civil Rights Movement. Honestly, I was kind of nervous at first because it’s a pretty big topic and I wanted to make sure I did it justice. 

So, getting ready for it took quite a bit of effort. I spent a few days researching—digging up articles, watching documentaries, and checking out books from the library. Then, I put together a PowerPoint with photos, timelines, and a few quotes to keep my classmates engaged. Oh, and I practiced in front of my mirror a couple times, just to get my words flowing and make sure I wouldn’t freeze up. 

When it came time to actually present, my heart was pounding, but once I got started, things just kind of clicked. I could see some people nodding along, which helped boost my confidence. By the end, I felt pretty relieved and proud of myself. So, all in all, it was a nerve-wracking experience at first, but it turned out to be really rewarding.
title:
Describe a time when you had to give a speech or presentation in class.
What was the topic of your presentation? 
How did you prepare for it? 
 How did you feel while you were giving it?

感受:  
"While I was giving the presentation, I felt [describe your feelings, such as nervous, excited, confident, anxious]."  
"At first, I [describe your initial reaction, e.g., 'was really nervous and my hands were shaking'], but as I continued, I [describe how your feelings changed, e.g., 'started to feel more comfortable']."  

总结:  
"In the end, the presentation went [describe the outcome, e.g., well, better than expected, or it taught me a lot]."
